sentences.text: Hi, Gabrielle.
Hey, how are you?
I'm good, how are you?
Good.
We should have… Team, I was lucky to get this many people with an open time.
I was… I'm gonna be honest, I was actually surprised.
How quickly you were able to book it with this amount of people.
So, nice work.
Thanks, yeah, I looked at it, and I was like, sorry about your lunch, guys.
Well, I appreciate it, thank you.
Hi, Steven.
Hello, how are you?
I'm good, how are you?
I'm doing well, thank you.
Hi, Kristen.
And then, Rebecca, did I see you on there?
Excuse me.
Right now, yeah.
Hey, everyone.
Hi, Rebecca.
Sorry, my computer's having a little bit of a meltdown, so…
Blazer.
Get my audio working, give me just a sec.
Yeah, I was gonna say, we're gonna have, kind of, team members from development and acquisition, so Steven's on development.
Kristen is… Justine's gonna be joining, and then Justin's on acquisitions, I think he's joining.
And then…
Rebecca's our solutions architect and guru of all things technology, but you can introduce yourself more if you want, Rebecca.
That's not good enough.
Did you say… Gabrielle, you said we're still waiting on Justin, correct?
Yeah, and there's Justina, I can't see.
No.
Okay.
Well, it's 12.
02 for the sake of time, why don't we just get rolling, because I think we have the majority.
Of course.
Well, I appreciate you guys all hopping on and connecting with us, and thank you for doing this during your lunch time, so that's a huge thank you.
We actually spoke with, Gabrielle last week in regards to Apartment IQ.
I'm not sure how much she filled you all in on what the tool is.
I've actually previously met with both Kristen and Alex Cox, who I understand is no longer at Buckingham.

About a year and a half ago, and at the time, it was really just geared towards the operational side for your stabilized portfolio, and one of the important things that Kristen had mentioned at that point was you really need a tool that caters to both the operational side of things, but also for your new business side, for both acquisitions as well as new development.
So, I'm happy to say that since then, we have built out the tool Tremendously.
So now it is geared towards everybody in the organization, from on-site teams up through senior leadership on both sides of the business.
Justin's our product expert.
He's gonna be going through the tool, with you all, but just super high-level data analytics tool, and we're providing information to all of your competitors.
We're only using publicly available data, and we're tracking close to 40 million units nationwide every single day.
And what's awesome about it is we can track any property out there, they don't have to be a customer of ApartmentIQ, they just essentially need to have a website and utilize online leasing.
There are three, sections within ApartmentIQ that Justin will go through.

This first section is called My Property, so this is gonna be for the day-to-day operations for your stabilized portfolio, so you can look at how your units and floor plans are positioned within each specific comp set.
The second section's called Research, so this tool allows you to generate a market survey for any property nationwide that you don't currently own or manage, so great for that acquisitions purpose.

But then also, within, research, you can actually create a custom property, so if you have a new development project, you can enter that in, and we'll generate a market survey for that project that you have.
And then the third section is called Explore, and this is brand new.
This is going to give a high-level overview of market and sub-market data down to specific neighborhoods, and within Explore, we're also providing new supply data and demographic information as well.
Gabrielle had shared some of your current tools, being CoStar, Yardi Matrix, Helodata for the operational side of things.
I'm just curious to hear anybody's feedback in regards to the tools that you're currently using.
Things that might be missing, things that you like, don't like, and then, we can go from there and just show you the platform.
I guess, Kristen, not to put you on the spot, but I understand that your team has the most experience utilizing Hello Data.
Is there anything within that tool, that might be missing for you all, or things that you do really like?

Yeah, I mean, I think with any of these tools and platforms, like, key information for us, or the things that we are either looking at on a project-by-project basis or market basis, is, you know, good Rent information, things like lease-up velocity, especially on our development side, so seeing where markets are leasing up and what that sort of progression is.
understanding concessions that are taking place in the market, and sort of outlining what those concessions are.
You know, CoStar uses a percentage, Hello Data, actually, it gives you an effective rent, but it also calls out for you what the current concession is based on the scraping they have available.
we want to be able to see the historic information, as well as future projections for a market.

And I think part of that is, and one of the challenges we have right now, which, I do think maybe HelloData's reporting does a little bit better than what we can pull out of CoStar, is what's an easy and efficient way for us to be pulling information on a competitive set.
And being able to see That historical information of where the rents have been versus where, you know, they currently sit today, and then being able to utilize what are potential future projections.
So I think it's a little bit of everything.
And then a platform that also allows us to see, like, operating efficiencies, OPEX in a market.
What are some areas or challenges in going into new markets that maybe we haven't operated in before that are things that we'd be able to see based on the information available?

So, what I would say is we are always looking for What is the easiest and most efficient way to compile the data that's available to us so that we can be making the best decisions, whether that's Gabrielle looking at rent levels and trying to determine what markets we can go into based on, you know, where our costs are coming in at versus when we're trying to compare our current portfolio properties versus how the market is performing.
And then that plays into how we budget moving forward.
That makes sense.
Thank you.
I would just, I'll just quickly piggyback off of KJ's notes on, you know, going back to get historical information, right?

Like, I'm sure you can all appreciate the fact that, yes, a data point starts to tell a story, but if we're able to go back, you know, and capture some of the historical rents and how they've evolved.
whether with the market projections or against the market projections, I think that that's helpful.
And then I'll also add in one other caveat.
It would be great if there was an automated way to do these.

Like, it's very labor-intensive to go into CoStar and pull these Market reports, and make sure that we as team members are doing them consistently, so… Automation, historical, would be big for… from where I sit.
Great.
Anybody else before we get into Apartment IQ?
Okay, well, I'm gonna hand it over to Justin.
I think that based on what you all shared, I think that Apartment IQ can fit a lot of those needs for you all.
as he's going through the tool, we want this to be interactive, so feel free to hop in with any questions, as he's going through things.
We don't just want to be talking at you, we want it to be more conversational.

So yeah, I'll hand it over to him, and at the end of the call, we can decide whether or not this is something that you guys want to continue to explore, or if you've seen what you need to see, and that's it.
So, I appreciate your time today.
Justin?
Yeah, and I'll mention, just in regards to historicals, so we've been collecting unit-level data for the past 5 years, so we'll have up to 5 years' worth of historical data.
And our coverage should be substantially better than what you're getting with CoStar.
We actually had a, one of our enterprise customers, they tracked, like, 600 subject properties, plus 5 to 7 comps for each of those, those subject properties.
We had 99% coverage for every property they were looking to track.

Costar was at, like, 85% or 86%, so I was surprised that the coverage was that low, but, like Jamie said, we can track any property out there that does have an online presence, so it should literally be able to give you complete coverage of anywhere that you're interested in.
But yeah, I'm gonna take you to the product.
I'm actually gonna kind of start from, the, I guess, just a different part.
So I'm gonna actually start with Explore, and this is gonna be all of that market, sub-market data.
So, this would be more of that CoStar replacement, and what's nice about this is, I think, and I might be biased, but I believe we have the best rent comp data in the industry.
But as you'll see here, in addition to the RENCOM data, we fill in a lot of those gaps that you need for a CoStar subscription.
So, we'll have new construction supply pipeline data, we have demographics, we'll have ownership information, transactional data, building specs, cap rates.
you know, we have just about everything here.
I think what's exciting, too, is… I'll show you, we just added forecasting.
So, I think that our goal with this is to kind of be that one-stop shop for everything multifamily, and I think over the last year, 18 months, we've really built up to that point.
So, with Explore here, what's nice is you can type in, again, any city, town, neighborhood, zip code, county, you can draw your own boundary if you'd like.
I'm based in Phoenix, so I always use Phoenix.
But what's nice about this, for example, is in the Phoenix MSA, we're tracking about 500,000 units.
We're going to have every filtering option that you're used to in a CoStar.
So if you want to look at vintage, or maybe you only care about those lease-ups or Class A properties, you know, you can slice and dice this data however you'd like.
So, you want to look at underperforming assets, or maybe properties that have, you know, two bedrooms between X dollar per foot and X dollar per foot.
Again, you can slice and dice this to really hone in on exactly what you do care about.
And then from here, what we're gonna do is we're gonna, one, give you the rent comp data.
So if I click into here.
I'm going to show you first historical rent growth.
This can be looked at monthly, quarterly, yearly.
You can look at both gross and net rent.
If you keep scrolling, I'm going to give you a 30-day snapshot, which is going to include performance metrics and averages broken out by unit type.
And then in regards to historicals, so again, we give you the 5 years' worth of historical data that we've been tracking right here in the application.
So, inside of here, this is going to include rent trends, both gross and net.
It will include supply by bed type, Leasing velocity by bed type.
And also those occupancy, lease, and exposure percentage trends.
If I go back, if I keep scrolling down then, what else you get is going to be that new supply information.
So from here, if I click into it, you know, I can show you how many units and properties make up each stage of construction.
When they're slated to be delivered, so we can give you the forward-looking supply, as well as if you want to look at historical, you know, new supply releases, you can do that as well.
And then we'll give you details on the, the sizes of the project.
How are you guys capturing or aggregating that information?
Are you working with contacts with the… developers themselves, or…
Yeah, good question.
So, we have, 3 different sources for construction and new supply.
So, one being building permits.
Two, we have a third-party, partnership with a group called Build Central.
They specialize in the space.

And then we do have a small human-led research team on our side that looks at, like, news publications or press releases, and that's honestly to make sure that we're moving the project to the appropriate stages.
So, a combination of those three, those three sources is where our supply data comes from.
Okay.
Good to know, thanks.
Yep.
And I know it's gonna be asked, so can you… you can filter these by, to BTR, correct?
You can, yeah, absolutely.
So, we have what we consider to be the largest definitive BTR database in the entire industry.

So, absolutely, yeah, we have, as you can see here at the top, that's part of the filtering offering, so conventional BTR, student, senior, affordable filters are actually coming, by the end of the month.
And then, I'm actually going to get a little bit more granular than the MSA here, but what's nice is we also include demographics.
So this will have population, education, income, and employment data.

And Gabrielle, I think I mentioned this last time, but what I've heard that's unique about our demographics compared to other providers is that we can actually take them down to the actual neighborhood or custom-drawn boundaries.
So, rather than getting just the market or sub-market demographics, you can actually get that specific area that you do care about.
What else I like, too, is, like, if I bounce over to Scottsdale, for example, I can click this property tab, and I can show you a list of every property that meets your criteria in the defined area.
So, a different view for the property.
But what else is helpful is if you click into a property here, if I click into V-tree, so I can give you, first of all, property, unit, and amenity-specific images.
Over here on the right, this is where I can show you ownership information, ownership history, transactional data, and those building specs.
In regards to comparing performance, so down below here, I can let you look at, rent, exposure, those concessions, as well as leasing velocity performance, over whatever timeframe you care about.
I think what else is helpful is down below here, we let you compare the subject property, which is V3 in this example, versus its top competitors, and then the market as a whole.
If you keep scrolling, we're gonna lay out unit mix really well.
So we can obviously show you the 1, 2s, and threes, but if you want, you can expand this and see each unique floor plan within each bed group.
Over here on the right, you can actually look at rent performance for each floor plan over whatever time frame you want as well.

We do include an estimated rent roll, so what's great about this, again, is the fact that we've been tracking this unit-level data, every day for the last 5 years is that in most instances, we've seen every unique floor plan, or every unique unit within a property, so we can back into this, this rent roll.
And what's great here is if I click into it, I can show you the status of every, you know, every unit in the property.

So I can show you if it's available or if it's under application, but if it is leased, I can show you the date and the dollar amount that it leased for, what concession was attached to it, how many days it's out on the market.
I can also show you things like lost at least percentage.
And I can show you how long a resident's been in that actual unit.
So this is… this is residence tenure by month.
So, really easy to kind of reconstruct that rent roll right from this view.
If I keep scrolling, what's great is I give you a light version of a market survey.
So in a second, I'll give you kind of the more in-depth version of what our market surveys look like, but this is that light version of, again, V-Tree and its direct competitors.
You can easily compare performance, you know, Ren's performance metrics, etc.

And I'll let you know, just from this view right here, our system will default to pull in what we consider to be the best 5 or 6 comps, but if… in a different view, you have the full ability to add or remove, you know, as many as you'd like.
Show and continue on, I can show you transaction history.
That'll include cap rates.
Justin, real quick question.
Yep.

How do I then save this search, or is there a way to then go back to what… I've generated or created, or is this one of those platforms where you have to go in and basically generate a new one each time?
So, like, if you, like, if you… are you talking, like, if we use Vitri as an example, like, you want all the market data for V3, you want to save it?

Well, I want to be able to say, okay, I want to go back into this in a month, and I want to pull the information again, but I don't have time, so I want someone else on the team to be able to go and pull it.
Like, is there a saved search type area that we can share amongst team members, or anything like that?
Yeah, good question.
So right now, there's, like, a saved search for a specific property, so, like, if you wanted all this rent comp data on VTree in a month, we can do that.
If you say, hey, I want all this saved search data for Scottsdale, Arizona in a month, you have to recreate it.
But we are releasing, like, a saved search, or even if, like, your team has specific boundaries that you'd like custom-drawn, being able to save those.
So that would be a feature that would be released before you guys would ever receive an invoice.
Yep.
Yep, good question.
Just in regards to transaction history, so coverage should be really good here.
We have everything going back to 2002, that transacted for over $2.
5 million.
Just to kind of finish this up, then, if you keep scrolling, we will show you new supply within whatever area or radius you care about.
And then we'll also give you those demographic trends, so 1, 3, 5 mile trends regarding your more popular demographics.
So, this is kind of that new Explore tool, so market, sub-market data.
What else I'm excited about is going to be this market comparison dashboard.
And, Kristen, this kind of goes in line with what you're talking about, you know, understanding market performance, and then also maybe looking for new markets to move into, or markets to invest in.
So, for my example right here, I just have the top 50 MSAs in the country pulled up, but you can create this dashboard to reflect however you want.
Maybe you're moving into Texas, you want to track, you know, 20 different zip codes in Texas, or 20 different cities across the country.
You can lay this out however you'd like.
The data can also be aggregated over whatever timeframe you care about as well.
And then, as I'll show you here in a second, I've got, like, 30 different metrics, kind of giving you the high level of literally everything you should care about in these markets.

So, we'll give you the rent comp data, we'll give you performance metrics, we'll even show things like supply, what's under construction, we can color code rent growth, so green and red, color coding, but we'll give you sales volume, we'll give you market data, that'll also include your demographics here in regards to population growth, and things like household income, etc.

So, this is something we built that's like, again, just a way to keep a pulse on the market, look for certain investment opportunities, or just look at general trends over whatever markets you might care about.
this is all… I mean, everything I'm showing you, just so you're aware, too, is exportable to CSV or Excel.
So, any questions here?
Pretty straightforward with that.
Cool.
Just a reminder, then, as far as the operations side goes, we have this My Property section.
This is where your market surveys would live.
So, each one of your properties would get their own individual property card.
All of the data is based off of their specific comp set.
As a reminder, your team's in complete control of your comp set, so if you'd like, you can provide us with a list of comps that you're interested in, we can set it up that way.

Our system does have a comp matching algorithm as well, so we can make some suggestions or recommendations, but again, you can always add or remove a property to your comp set directly in the application anytime you'd like.
But you click into here, and just a reminder, the data is updated as of this morning, but what's nice is we do give you the ability to aggregate it over whatever time frame you are interested in.
We have both PDF and Excel reports that can be downloaded anytime you'd like.
They can also be automated to be delivered to an inbox on whatever frequency or cadence you'd like as well.
Down here on the left, all of these metrics are going to be sortable from high to low, or low to high.
And then in the top right, this is where you'd actually go to edit your comps.
So you click into here, we make it really easy.

On the right, you get a map view of the area, the blue pin would be your property, white pins are properties already added to your comp set, black pins are properties that are in the area that you might want to consider adding as well.
Major call-out over here on the left in the list view is going to be this comp score that we do assign to every property in the vicinity of yours.
It's a correlation score, but essentially a reference point in terms of who our system thinks is a good competitor.
And this should be objectively or, you know, directionally correct.
These scores are based off of things like distance or proximity, vintage, total units, pricing, and amenity matching.
And this is the spot that we can also, for, like, a piece of land or potential new development, that we can pull in comps and do the same thing and generate those market summaries, right?
That'll be under research, but same idea.
Yeah, I think that one's of big interest to kind of this group, if we want to go through that one, because a lot of us are using that.
For sure.
Yeah, so under research, so this is geared towards new, for acquisition or for, or new development, but what we let you do here is pretty much query our entire database.

So just so you're aware, just quickly from the acquisition side, all you would do is click New Report, you type in the name or the address of whatever property out there you care about, you find it in the list, you click on it, and in 3 seconds, you have all of this market data.
From the dune development side, what we let you do is actually create a custom property.

So if you have a new development in mind, you can type in the cross streets, if you already own the plot of land or know the plot of land, or if you just say, hey, I want to develop in Scottsdale, Arizona, you can type in Scottsdale.
And then you can put in, you know, your bill, your total units, and then you can build out your unit mix.
So if you know you want to have 1, 2s, and threes, the averages of each, and the rent price you're trying to achieve.
Based off of those metrics, our system generates this market survey for you based off of who we think, you know, the top competitors would be based off of your hypotheticals there.
Same idea, though.
Like, again, these are system-recommended comps, but with that edit comp button.
You have full control of whoever you add, remove, in regards to those comps.
So, the way this is laid out, for example, I'm going to toggle on a couple other metrics, just to make sure we're hitting on everything.
you can see with this dropdown here, there's quite a few metrics that I don't have toggled on, but let's nail a few of them.
And just as… I think a big thing here too, Kristen, just in regards to, like, Hello Data, like, those guys don't show occupancy.
We do.
I think it's worth mentioning that one of the big fundamental differences with us compared to a HelloData is going to be our source.
So, for some context, we scrape 1.
1 million property websites every single night.
They're, their website says that they scrape about a quarter million property websites every single night.
So what that means is that they actually source the majority of their data from ILSs, like Apartments.
com, and the issue there is what you see is what you get.
And that's in regards to properties that are hiding availability.
So whether it be for a marketing tactic or lease-ups, which were mentioned earlier, you know, you can't get performance metrics without accurate availability.

So with us, the reason we can is because we go to the property websites, our scrapers are sophisticated enough to get around hidden availability, thus us giving you occupancy and better and better performance metrics as a whole.
So…
To that question, let me ask that then.

Does this have the ability that If I wanted to have this on a recurring… Monday report for, you know, my Buckingham-owned portfolio, because I wanted to look from a revenue management standpoint to make sure that our rents each week are moving in LRO, as I would anticipate that they're supposed to.
The system allows me to send that email out, like, the report, like, emailed to me once a week, so I don't have to go in and run it.
Yeah, absolutely, yeah.
Yep, we can get you an Excel report of that.
You can, if you want it delivered on a, you know, weekly on a Monday, too, whatever you want, we can automate that for you, for sure.
Yep.

Yeah, and I think, in general here, what's nice is… and this is… you know, just a public data source in general, but I think that any metric data point calculation that you're seeing here, I can trace back to the source.
So there's no guessing on where it came from, I mean, we can back every data point up, and what else I like is that you can drill into all these metrics as well.
So if you want to see the 19 units that are available at the Palladium, you can jump into here and see those 19 units that are available.
Or if you want to see those 7 units that leased the Palladium over the last 30 days, you can see those 7 units.
So, really easy to audit the data, very easy to trace it back to the source.
And two other things here I think that's important, because they were brought up, is one, concessions.
So, concessions, we've mastered.
So, pop-ups, ribbons, banners, mouseovers, images, we can account for them all.

even if they're property, I'm sorry, even if they're floor plan or unit-specific, our LLM, our AI that we use to go out there, it's perfected collecting them, and then taking it a step further and calculating your net effective rents and net effective price per feet, all the way down to the unit level.
So, perfect coverage in regards to concessions.
And then also a, A smaller note that I'll talk about here is, in regards to these performance metrics, is going to be leasing velocity.
So, a group like Hello Data, and I think they might have just changed this from what I saw, but they used to basically say that, hey, if a unit leaves a property website, it's a lease.
And that's not the case.
You have to account for applications and cancel denials.
So, they were always overstating leasing velocity.
You can see here that we do account for applications, we do account for cancels and denials, so our leasing velocity was always, you know, much, much, much more accurate compared to groups like that.

I will show trends too, so again, in regards to trends here, you can pick and choose your time frame, so again, you get all 5 years included here, you can pick your interval, and then you can look at the data at the property level, we're broken out by floor plan.
But inside of here, we're going to give you rent trends, so both gross and net.
You can compare your subject property, or your new development, compared to the meeting of the comp set.
Or if you'd like, you can toggle over and get each individual's property's performance over that given time frame as well.
We also include concession history, which can be looked at as a percentage or as a value.
We have supply, leasing velocity.
And then also those occupancy, lease, and exposure percentage trends.

And I believe, were you telling me that on the market summary, we can, if it's our theoretical development property, we can, like, show where we would have rents there, and it can compare to where the comps are now in chart form?
Yeah, exactly, yeah.
Automatically.
You mean, like, right, like, in this thing?
Yeah, or when you do the export to Excel, because that would be… That's gonna automate a lot of, I think, what our normal market summary does, if we can put it in here and it can pop it out.
So maybe at the end, if you want to show what that export looks like, that'd be great.
I can show down.
Yeah, so this export, I think I think I got what you're looking for, but, like, the export here, so again, this is… you can download this whenever you'd like.
Just quickly, it'll include that market survey that I walked you through, so nice, clean, professional look to it.
It has floor plan level record… I'm sorry, bed, bed type level reporting, so you get the averages at each property.
Then we can show you each unique floor plan within a competitive set.
What else I like is that we include all of the raw unit-level data behind what's powering these reports.
So this is every unit ever associated with your property and a property in your comp set.
So if you want to further manipulate it, create pivot tables, etc, easy to do so.

And then, Gabrielle, I think this is what you were referencing here, I think, but you can look at this stat at the property level, we're broken out by floor plan, but it's going to include about a dozen different charts breaking out every major KPI you'd be interested in.
And did you say this kind of report template can be customizable, so we can export what we want and have that auto-run?
Yeah, that's exactly right.
So, in regards to the, the customization, Any kind of filtering?
Which, there's quite a few filters here.
Any kind of filtering, sorting, or any kind of, like, timestamp that you hear… that you select here will be carried over to that export.

So if you only want to include certain metrics, you only want to include certain comps, or, you know, maybe from the development side, you want to look at this over the last 6 months instead of based off of yesterday, you can pick and choose that level of customization that will carry you over to the, To the export.
But Justin, to confirm, so we have a way to, in essence, create a location, which would be our development location.
And we could put in what we are projecting, our unit mix, sizing, rents, all of that, what it would be.
And then when we run a market summary report for it, it will… Then tie into there based on that.
Yep, exactly.
Yeah.
Yeah, so, like, right here, like, for my basic example, I just want, twos, and threes.
If you want to have 5 different styles of 1s, 5 different styles of twos, 5 different styles of threes, you can get as granular as you want with actually building your subject property.
a way to, when we're placing these in on the front end, identify them as being just BTR, or multifamily, or student, or whatever that is?
Yeah, so what you would do, like, the one I created for my example was just in Scottsdale.
You can rename it whatever you want, though.
You could say Scottsdale BTR Reporter, you know, you can call it whatever you want.
Yep.

And also, for what it's worth, too, I think, back to this Explore page, Rather than just getting this data on, like, a market or sub-market level, you can actually type in the name or the address of a property out there, and we can give you all of this data based off of a certain radius around that specific property.
So, you know, you can get… you can even do it, like, around points of interest.
Like, if you say, hey, I want all this market data around, you know, a college or university, you know, you can… you can pick and choose however this is laid out.
And, rent… I'm sorry, forecasting would be right here.
Let me see if I have this talk turned on for my account, just to show it to you.
See if I can get Dallas, actually.
So what's exciting with forecasting is we, we brought over the team from CoStar.
Yeah, I don't have it turned on.
We brought over the team from CoStar about 3 months ago, and their first thing that they're releasing here is going to be forecasting.
It's going to be, the first release is going to be the top 150 MSAs in the country, and it'll be 5 years forward-looking.
So, forecasting's something that you'd have included here as well, and it would just be kind of laid out in this view here.
